title: Glurrg
content:

{{Quote|Bring mesa five food animals and mesa might be convincen to given yousa some worker glurrgs.|Captain Marsune, to Boss Gallo|audio=GN1-WCMRG-L96.flac|<ref name="GB" />}}
'''Glurrgs''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]], bipedal creatures that were employed as workers for the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] civilization of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. Although glurrgs typically did not communicate in [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]], they were skilled in a variety of areas, including [[Farm/Legends|farming]], construction, [[Fishing/Legends|fishing]], [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]], and [[Mining/Legends|mining]] [[Ore/Legends|ores]] and [[Nova crystal/Legends|nova crystals]] for use in building and maintaining Gungan [[City/Legends|settlements]]. Besides being capable of harvesting [[Carbon/Legends|carbon]] from [[Tree/Legends|trees]] for conversion into [[Duraplast/Legends|duraplast]] at [[carbon processing center]]s, glurrgs were efficient laborers who compared favorably with the [[Labor droid/Legends|labor droids]] used by contemporaneous civilizations.

Glurrgs were a pivotal part of Gungan society since at least [[3000 BBY]]. During the [[War of the Gungan tribes]], the creatures were used as laborers by all of the Gungan tribal factions involved in the conflict. Glurrgs continued to contribute valuable labor to Gungan society up to and during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] cartel. Glurrgs helped construct a [[Gungan Temple|monument]] at the [[Liberation of Spinnaker|recapture of Spinnaker]] to rally Gungan forces against the invading [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Trade Federation Droid Army]] and assisted in the [[Liberation of Harte Secur]], which drew [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] forces away from Naboo's [[City/Legends|capital city]] of [[Theed/Legends|Theed]] in a diversionary assault.

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:Glurrgupclose.jpg|thumb|left|A Gungan glurrg]]
Glurrgs were a [[Color/Legends|tan]]-skinned, bipedal [[Species/Legends|species]] that generally stood shorter than<ref name="GB" /> 1.9 [[Meter/Legends|meters]], the average height of their [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] masters, the [[Water/Legends|aquatic]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] species.<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Alien Species">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref> Their upper pair of appendages did not end in defined [[Limb/Legends|limbs]] with individual digits, but instead resembled fins or flippers. Despite this, they could still handle machinery and [[Farm/Legends|farm]] implements without the need of fingers or opposable thumbs. They could swim, even in deep waters, and were proficient at [[Fishing/Legends|fishing]]. Glurrgs possessed two [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], which protruded above their heads on [[Ommatophora|stalks]]. Glurrgs stood and walked in a hunched-over stance, propping their arms against their legs when idle. Glurrgs had slender thighs, but their lower legs were bulkier, with broad, oblong feet. Other characteristic features of glurrgs were a rounded, squat body, thin forelimbs, and a large [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]]. They were also known for having frail constitutions.<ref name="GB" /> Glurrgs were [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]],<ref name="CSWE" /> although they were capable workers that could use a variety of implements to perform their tasks.<ref name="GB" />

==Behavior and intelligence==
Glurrgs were native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], where they played a key role in Gungan society. Their labor skills meant that they were used to construct and gather resources for many Gungan [[City/Legends|settlements]]. Large amounts of technology or resources were not required to sustain the glurrg population at a given settlement; they required merely food to eat and sufficient shelter in which to live.<ref name="GB" />

Glurrgs were adept in a wide range of skills, including [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] creatures such as [[Shaak/Legends|shaaks]], [[Fish/Legends|fishing]], repairing Gungan war machinery and buildings, farming, foraging for [[Muja fruit/Legends|muja fruit]], harvesting [[Carbon/Legends|carbon]] from [[Tree/Legends|trees]] and mineral deposits for conversion into [[Duraplast/Legends|duraplast]] at [[carbon processing center]]s, and mining for [[Ore/Legends|ore]] and [[Nova crystal/Legends|nova crystals]]. Being versatile laborers, glurrgs were skilled enough to swiftly change between their various tasks upon command. When hunting, unlike Gungan [[Soldier/Legends|warriors]], glurrgs used small [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] rather than [[Booma/Legends|booma]]-based or similar weaponry, although they did not use such weapons in self-defense. Glurrgs could construct any building required by the Gungans, whether they were simple [[prefab shelter]]s, sizable [[Fortress/Legends|fortresses]], or [[command center]]s. Despite their relatively basic role in Gungan society, glurrgs were able to use technology such as [[Beamdrill/Legends|beamdrills]] and heavy [[carbon extractor]]s, and to learn the blueprints of increasingly more sophisticated buildings over [[Time/Legends|time]]. As such, their efforts were invaluable to the Gungan military arm, the [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|Gungan Grand Army]], particularly when constructing a new base or outpost. Without glurrgs, the Gungans would have been hard-pressed to collect enough resources from the surrounding environment to maintain a war effort and construct additional structures.<ref name="GB" />

Glurrgs were not used for construction of Gungan underwater settlements; Gungans instead preferred to use [[utility trawler]] vehicles for the creation of buildings beneath the surface of Naboo's [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]]. Furthermore, glurrgs were not well suited to combat—their relatively frail constitution and lack of [[Armor/Legends|armor]] and self-defense abilities limited them to attacking a target with their bare fins.<ref name="GB" />

Glurrgs communicated audibly through chirps, whistles, and grunts, but they could not speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] or the [[Gungan (language)|Gunganese]] dialect of Basic.<ref name="GB" />

==History==
[[File:OtohSancture.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Glurrgs at Otoh Sancture]]
Glurrgs were domesticated by the Gungans<ref name="CSWE" /> by the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3000 BBY]],<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> over two millennia prior to the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]].<ref name="CSWE" /> One settlement that employed glurrgs as the primary source of labor in 3000 BBY was [[Otoh Sancture]], a [[City/Legends|city]] governed by the Gungan [[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Gallo/Legends|Gallo]]. When the village was [[Destruction of Otoh Sancture|destroyed]] by carnivorous [[Bursa/Legends|bursa]] creatures incited by Gallo's rival, Boss [[Rogoe/Legends|Rogoe]], Gallo was left stranded in the woods, lacking resources save for five [[Nerf/Legends|nerfs]] that he had captured. Gallo traded the nerfs to a local leader named [[Marsune (Gungan)|Marsune]] in exchange for worker glurrgs, then tasked the creatures with the construction of a new outpost and the harvest of materials needed for the [[Army/Legends|army]] that he would later use to retake Otoh Sancture.<ref name="GB" />

Glurrgs remained a valuable source of labor for Gungan factions throughout the conflict denoted as the [[War of the Gungan tribes]]. They were used by both Gallo and Rogoe during the conflict's [[Raid on Spearhead|Battle of Spearhead]] for constructing buildings and harvesting resources, as well as by the neutral [[Bongomeken Collective]] manufacturing group, which later provided seagoing [[bongo]] vessels to Gallo. Gallo ultimately prevailed over Rogoe. The ubiquitous glurrg remained in use by Gungans for at least the next three thousand years.<ref name="GB" />

When the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] cartel known as the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] invaded Naboo in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] with its [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Droid Army]], glurrgs were still employed for construction and collection, much as they had been at the rise of Gungan civilization. Boss [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Rugor Nass]], the leader of the Gungans of the settlement [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]] at the time, used a group of glurrg workers in his [[Liberation of Spinnaker|attack]] on the Federation-occupied city of [[Spinnaker/Legends|Spinnaker]]. In this assault, the glurrgs not only provided the resources to keep Nass's army fighting against the Trade Federation, but they also constructed a [[Gungan Temple]] monument on the ruins of a [[Neimoidian Throne]], a Trade Federation monument that the Gungan Grand Army destroyed in a bid to rally more Gungans to Nass's aid.<ref name="GB" />

In the events leading up to the conflict's [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Grassy Plains]], glurrgs were employed in the [[Liberation of Harte Secur|liberation]] of another key occupied town, [[Harte Secur]], as well as the freeing of [[Fambaa/Legends|fambaa]] beasts confined nearby. As Nass amassed a Gungan army with which to attack the town, glurrgs provided resources such as ore, carbon, food, and nova [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]] for the war effort, which was aimed at drawing the Trade Federation's [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] army away from the Naboo capital city of [[Theed/Legends|Theed]].<ref name="GB" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Glurrg_kiss.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Concept art of a Gungan kissing a glurrg]]
Glurrgs only appear in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'', in which they fulfill the worker role for the Gungan civilization, with similar behavior as that of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[ASP-7 labor droid/Legends|ASP-7 labor droids]] or [[Royal House of Naboo/Legends|Royal House of Naboo]] [[PKN-49 worker droid]]s. Unlike the majority of these civilizations, aside from the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]], Gungan glurrgs are biological in nature rather than [[Droid/Legends|droid]]-based. The release of ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' in [[2008]] confirms that they are non-sentient.

Glurrgs resemble concept art provided for Gungans on the [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]], as drawn by [[Terryl Whitlatch]].<ref name="DB Gungan">{{DB|species|gungan|Gungan}} {{C|[https://web.archive.org/web/20020314153418im_/http://starwars.com/databank/species/gungan/img/bts_bg.jpg "Behind the scenes" section image not referenced after 2008 Databank reformatting]}}</ref> The same concept art was later used in [[2012]] as the basis for the [[Zilkin/Legends|Zilkin]] character [[Meebur Gascon/Legends|Meebur Gascon]], who made his first appearance in "[[Secret Weapons]]," an episode of the [[The Clone Wars: Season Five|fifth season]] of the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series.<ref name="Secret Weapons Episode Guide">{{SW|url=series/clone-wars/secret-weapons-episode-guide|text="Secret Weapons" Episode Guide | The Clone Wars}}</ref>
